channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: House rejects Trump-backed plan on government shutdown, leaving next steps uncertain
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A day before a potential government shutdown, the House resoundingly rejected President-elect Donald Trump's new plan to fund operations and suspend the debt ceiling, as Democrats and dozens of Republicans refused to accommodate his sudden demands.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shutdown threat looms after stop-gap spending bill fails on House floor
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump and his newest top-lieutenant, Elon Musk, have sent Washington scrambling to avoid a government shutdown, even before Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: Susan Davis, Deirdre Walsh, Claudia Grisales, Elena Moore, and Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: Johnson says plan C reached to avert shutdown, vote expected
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said Republicans have arrived at a plan C to avert a shutdown and the House will vote Friday morning on the legislation. "Yeah, yeah, we have a plan," Johnson said Friday morning as he entered the Capitol. "We're expecting votes this morning, so you all stay tuned.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump was poised to inherit a strong economy. Then things got rocky and he added to the uncertainty
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — The smooth economy that Donald Trump was poised to inherit suddenly looks a bit rockier — with critics saying the president-elect is contributing to the uncertainty. The Dow Jones stock index essentially ended Thursday flat after having posted 10 days of losses.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas House panel may never hear Robert Roberson’s testimony after new legal move
item.description: Attorney General Ken Paxton sought to delay legal proceedings until Jan. 13 — the day before the committee disbands — even as lawmakers vowed to continue fighting to hear from Roberson.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Kayla Guo, KPRC 2

item.title: Internal divisions doom Michigan Democrats in their final days of legislative control
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Michigan Democrats, recently hailed as a model of electoral and legislative success, are ending their final days in complete control of the state government mired by divisions and outright rebellion that has stalled votes on key priorities.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT and JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden-Harris admin rolls out another $4.28 billion in student loan handouts
item.description: As the Biden administration enters its final days, it has announced another $4.28 billion in student loan handouts for nearly 54,900 public workers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top DOGE senator to demand lame-duck Biden agencies halt costly telework talks, citing voter mandate
item.description: Senate DOGE leader Joni Ernst of Iowa will demand federal agency heads halt negotiations on telework and taxpayer-funded union time during the lame-duck Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US diplomats and hostage envoy in Syria on first visit since Assad ouster
item.description: The first U.S. diplomats to visit Syria since President Bashar Assad's ouster earlier this month are in Damascus to hold talks with the country's new leadership and seek information on the whereabouts of missing American journalist Austin Tice.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know about the debt ceiling debate as a government shutdown looms in Washington
item.description: A debate over the debt ceiling is at the center of a dispute over funding that is pushing Washington to the brink of a federal government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shutdown odds rapidly rise as House seeks plan B
item.description: The odds of a shutdown were rapidly growing on Thursday after President-elect Trump upended the talks by urging GOP lawmakers to shutter the government unless Democrats agree to a clean stopgap spending bill that raises or abolishes the debt limit.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICE deportations catch up to Trump-era numbers in FY 2024 as Biden admin comes to a close
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) deportations caught up with Trump-era levels in FY 2024, according to new agency data released on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here's what happens during a partial government shutdown
item.description: During a partial government shutdown, nonessential services pause and federal workers face furloughs or delayed pay, but essential operations will continue.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Closures, Social Security checks, furloughs: What a government shutdown might mean
item.description: Congress has until midnight Friday to come up with a way to fund the government, or federal agencies will shutter.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Veteran Fox News business anchor Neil Cavuto leaving after 28 years
item.description: Neil Cavuto, a business journalist who hosts a weekday afternoon show on Fox News Channel and has been with the network since its inception in 1996, is leaving after Thursday's show, Fox said.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans from hurricane-ravaged states warn against stripping disaster aid from spending bill
item.description: Senate Republicans from regions affected by hurricanes and other storms are warning negotiators against stripping disaster funding from the stopgap spending bill that is suddenly in turmoil after President-elect Trump heavily criticized Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) plan.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Musk demands Republicans sink spending bill
item.description: Musk whipped up outrage toward the bill and cheered on Republican lawmakers who announced their opposition.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: A power grab by Republicans in North Carolina becomes a referendum on democracy in the states
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Democrats in North Carolina were celebrating big wins in the swing state after the November election, including victories in races for governor and other top statewide offices. But the political high didn’t last long.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO and GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Spending bill contains relief for border farmers affected by lack of water
item.description: A spending bill that Congress is debating to continue funding the federal government past Friday also contains funding relief for farmers on the South Texas border who have been affected by a lack of water payments from Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Pornhub to block access in Florida amid lawsuit over new age verification law
item.description: Pornhub said it will block access to its website from Florida as an adult entertainment advocacy group sues over the state's new law requiring age verification.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Paris Hilton-backed youth welfare bill passes House, heads to Biden's desk
item.description: Today is a day I will never forget. After years of sharing my story and advocating on Capitol Hill, the Stop Institutional Child Abuse Act has officially passed the U.S. Congress,” Hilton said in a post on X.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Federal Reserve cuts its key rate by a quarter-point but envisions fewer reductions next year
item.description: The Federal Reserve cut its key interest rate by a quarter-point — its third cut this year — but also signaled that it expects to reduce rates more slowly next year than it previously envisioned, largely because of still-elevated inflation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Rand Paul blocks bill to authorize local, state authorities to track drones
item.description: Paul argued the bill would have expanded the government’s surveillance powers and downplayed concerns that the drones pose a threat to the public.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico tightens bonds with immigrant community in US
item.description: The government of Mexico is strengthening bonds with its citizens in the United States, pledging to assist them at a time immigrant communities could be coming under increased scrutiny here.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Government funding deal opens door to move Washington Commanders stadium to DC
item.description: The Washington Commanders could soon be kicking off in the heart of the nation’s capital. The government funding deal unveiled by congressional negotiators Tuesday paved the way for moving the Commanders' stadium from Maryland to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Angie Craig chosen as top Democrat on U.S. House Agriculture Committee
item.description: Minnesota 2nd District Rep. Angie Craig was elected Tuesday to serve as the top Democrat on the U.S. House Agriculture Committee.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: New York judge says Trump is not immune from hush money conviction
item.description: A spokesperson for President-elect Trump said the case should never have been brought, called for its immediate dismissal.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Florida GOP lawmaker files bill banning political flags in public buildings
item.description: If one state senator can shore up enough support in Tallahassee, political flags will no longer be allowed to be flown in public buildings.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge rules Trump's conviction withstands Supreme Court immunity decision
item.description: A New York judge upheld a jury’s verdict that convicted President-elect Trump of a felony, ruling the outcome of the hush money case can withstand the Supreme Court’s new test for presidential immunity.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Former FBI informant pleads guilty to fabricating Biden claims
item.description: A former FBI informant on Monday entered a guilty plea on four federal charges related to falsifying statements that in part spurred a GOP congressional investigation into the Biden family.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: House bill would ban 'unnecessary' genital surgeries for intersex foster youth
item.description: States receiving federal funding for foster care and adoption assistance programs would be required to prohibit certain surgeries for foster children born with intersex traits under legislation introduced Monday by Rep. Sylvia Garcia (D-Texas).
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams' former chief advisor expects to be indicted: lawyer
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ingrid Lewis-Martin, a former top aide to New York Mayor Eric Adams, expects to be indicted on criminal charges related to alleged improper gifts, her lawyer said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Donald Trump says he would consider pardoning NYC Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- President-elect Donald Trump on Monday said he would consider pardoning Mayor Eric Adams after his historic indictment on federal charges. "I think that he was treated pretty unfairly," Trump said at a press conference in Florida. "I would certainly look at it.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Speaker Johnson faces 'complicated' decision on crucial Rules Committee chair
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is barreling into a tricky — yet critical — decision over whom to appoint as the next chair of the House Rules Committee, a post that will set the tone for legislating and play a key role in keeping Republicans united in next year’s ultrathin majority.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmakers struggle to reach deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional negotiators are struggling to reach a deal to keep the government's lights on past Dec. 20. Text of a continuing resolution (CR) for a government funding package was expected Sunday ahead of the looming shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Adams' Chief Advisor Ingrid Lewis-Martin steps down
item.description: Editor's Note: The video above originally aired on Sept. 25, 2024. NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams’ Chief Advisor Ingrid Lewis-Martin has stepped down, sources told PIX11 News. Lewis-Martin on Sunday announced she's leaving her post.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Mayor Adams seeks to deport migrants who commit crimes
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams told President-elect Donald Trump’s border czar he would work with him to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in New York City. Adams met with Tom Homan at Gracie Mansion to discuss Trump’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11
